title: OpenClaw Gmail setup let a workspace .env CLOUDSDK_PYTHON select the Python interpreter for gcloud execution
description: During Gmail setup, OpenClaw runs gcloud via runGcloudCommand(), which derives its environment from gcloudEnv(). gcloudEnv() short-circuits and returns undefined whenever process.env.CLOUDSDK_PYTHON is already set, meaning gcloud inherits the ambient CLOUDSDK_PYTHON value. Because a workspace .env opened by a trusted operator is loaded into the process environment, an attacker-controlled CLOUDSDK_PYTHON entry in that .env determines which Python interpreter gcloud invokes, allowing setup to execute through an unintended (workspace-controlled) local Python path rather than a trusted interpreter.
